zotero.abstractNote: Kronecker-factored approximate curvature (K-FAC) is a popular second-order optimization method for neural networks. However, its original formulation is restricted to simple feed-forward architectures. While extensions to more complex layers exist, they are often heuristic or lack a unified theoretical framework. In this work, we provide a general framework for K-FAC that covers a wide range of modern neural network architectures, including residual connections, normalization layers, and attention mechanisms. We achieve this by deriving the K-FAC approximation for general computational graphs. Our approach is modular, easy to implement, and recovers existing K-FAC variants as special cases. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our method on various architectures, including ResNets and Transformers, where it achieves competitive performance compared to first-order methods while requiring significantly fewer iterations.
zotero.conferenceName: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 36 (NeurIPS 2023)

extra.Editors: A. Oh and T. Neumann and A. Globerson and K. Saenko and M. Hardt and S. Levine

zotero.proceedingsTitle: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 36 (NeurIPS 2023)
